classification_rationale: Phase-1 scaffolding repo for AFI agent templates/manifests/spawning, not a protocol-defining or replay-critical component. README.md:7 "where agent templates are registered, versioned, and spawned across the AFI Protocol"; README.md:33 "Phase 1 Scaffolding - Template registry and manifest are established. Agent spawning logic is in development." It contains no contracts, no vault/TSSD storage, no mint/receipt logic, no USS/CPJ ingest, and no determinism rules. schemas/index.ts holds TypeScript mirror interfaces that are explicitly DERIVED from canonical afi-config schemas (schemas/index.ts:8-9 "@see afi-config/schemas/analyst-config.schema.json") rather than being canonical itself, so the repo supports/consumes the protocol surface but does not own any normative artifact.
primary_purpose: afi-factory is a Phase-1 scaffolding repo providing AFI agent templates, a TypeScript template/analyst-config registry, and an agent manifest for registering, versioning, and spawning AFI agents (per README.md:7 and package.json description "AFI agent factory and templates").
protocol_touchpoints: DAG/pipeline (analyst enrichment-node topology config in schemas/index.ts + template_registry.ts)
schemas (TypeScript mirrors of afi-config analyst-config & enrichment-node schemas)
SDK/API (exported template_registry loaders and type guards)

reference_only_assumptions.quote: bridging afi-config schemas with afi-reactor orchestration.

contradictions.title: Duplicated/derived type defs risk drift from canonical afi-config schemas
contradictions.detail: schemas/index.ts re-declares AnalystConfig/EnrichmentNodeConfig as hand-written TypeScript interfaces that claim compatibility with afi-config JSON schemas but are a separate copy with no codegen/import linkage. Canonical schema (afi-config/schemas/analyst-config.schema.json) requires [analystId, enrichmentNodes]; the factory type guard isAnalystConfig matches the same, but any future schema change must be manually mirrored, creating a second source of truth for a protocol-adjacent dialect.
contradictions.evidence: schemas/index.ts:7-9 'These interfaces are strongly typed and compatible with the JSON schemas defined in afi-config/schemas/analyst-config.schema.json and afi-config/schemas/definitions/enrichment-node.schema.json.'

contradictions.title: Analyst topology hardcoded without pinned plugin/validator versions for replay
contradictions.detail: template_registry.loadAnalystConfig hardcodes 'froggy-analyst-node' v1.0.0 with a fixed enrichment-node DAG (technical-indicators -> pattern-recognition -> ai-ml, plus optional sentiment/news) but records only a top-level config version and no per-plugin pinned versions. Per determinism rule (3) replay requires pinned plugin/analyst/validator versions; an analyst-config that omits per-plugin version pins cannot by itself anchor a deterministic, challenge-ready replay. This is reference scaffolding, but if treated as the analyst-config source it would be under-specified for replay.
contradictions.evidence: template_registry.ts:26-74 analystId 'froggy-analyst-node' version '1.0.0' with enrichmentNodes plugin:'technical-indicators'/'ai-ml' and no version field per node

contradictions.title: Stale/inconsistent paths between docs, codex, and actual layout
contradictions.detail: AGENTS.md describes directories templates/, agents/, factory/ that do not exist; .afi-codex.json lists entrypoint 'src/template_registry.ts' but the file is at repo root template_registry.ts; agent_manifest.json template entries point all entries at 'template_registry.ts' while loadTemplate references './templates/validator.ts' and './templates/emitter.ts' which do not exist. Cosmetic but causes confusion for contributors/droids.

upstream: afi-core (per .afi-codex.json dependsOn and README Related Repositories)

recommended_action: clarify reference-vs-normative + tag stale: keep classified as SUPPORTING; (1) make schemas/index.ts explicitly generated-from / re-exported from afi-config to remove the second source of truth; (2) fix stale directory and entrypoint references (AGENTS.md, .afi-codex.json, loadTemplate paths); (3) if analyst-config templates here are ever to feed scoring, require per-plugin pinned versions for replay.

notable_findings.title: Repo is Phase-1 scaffolding, not droid-ready
notable_findings.evidence: .afi-codex.json:24 '"droid_ready": false'; README.md:33 'Phase 1 Scaffolding ... Agent spawning logic is in development.'; package.json:12 test script 'echo No tests yet && exit 0'
notable_findings.recommendation: Treat as supporting scaffolding; do not cite as a protocol-defining repo in cross-repo synthesis.

notable_findings.title: Analyst-config TS types are derived mirrors of canonical afi-config schemas (second source of truth)
notable_findings.evidence: schemas/index.ts:8-9 'defined in afi-config/schemas/analyst-config.schema.json and afi-config/schemas/definitions/enrichment-node.schema.json'; both canonical schemas confirmed present in sibling afi-config repo
notable_findings.recommendation: Generate or re-export these types from afi-config to avoid divergence; afi-config remains the normative owner.

notable_findings.title: Hardcoded 'froggy-analyst-node' enrichment DAG with no per-plugin version pinning
notable_findings.evidence: template_registry.ts:24-74 'froggy-analyst-node' version '1.0.0' enrichmentNodes [technical-indicators, pattern-recognition, sentiment, news, ai-ml] - nodes carry plugin id but no version
notable_findings.recommendation: If this config ever drives scored outputs, add pinned plugin/analyst versions per determinism rule (3); otherwise label clearly as illustrative reference.

notable_findings.title: schemas/README claims Zod-based schemas but index.ts contains only plain TS interfaces + manual type guards
notable_findings.evidence: schemas/README.md:3 'This folder contains *.ts files that define Zod-based schemas'; schemas/index.ts has no zod import, uses hand-written type guards (isAnalystConfig)
notable_findings.recommendation: Correct README or adopt zod; ensures validation expectations are accurate.

notable_findings.title: Stale directory/entrypoint references across AGENTS.md, .afi-codex.json, and loadTemplate
notable_findings.evidence: AGENTS.md:45-48 lists templates/ agents/ factory/ (absent); .afi-codex.json:6 'src/template_registry.ts' (file at root); template_registry.ts:6-7 returns './templates/validator.ts','./templates/emitter.ts' (absent)
notable_findings.recommendation: Tag stale; reconcile paths with actual repo layout.

notable_findings.title: No commitment/evidence/analytics storage and no contracts present (correctly out of scope for this repo)
notable_findings.evidence: grep for mongodb|TSSD|vault|mint|beneficiary|epoch|BigQuery|kafka|postgres|.sol returned only generic 'secure vaults' (AGENTS.md:57) and 'manifests are contracts' (AGENTS.md:58); no storage/contract code
notable_findings.recommendation: None; confirms repo does not collapse planes and holds no on-chain/vault canon.

notable_findings.title: Repo is governed by external AFI Droid Charter (authority lives elsewhere)
notable_findings.evidence: AGENTS.md:5 'All agents ... must follow afi-config/codex/governance/droids/AFI_DROID_CHARTER.v0.1.md. If this AGENTS.md conflicts with the Charter, the Charter wins.'
notable_findings.recommendation: None; charter and codex governance are normative in afi-config, not here.
